title: Agence competitive positioning vs. AutoGen, CrewAI, MetaGPT, LangGraph, OpenHands, Cursor, Copilot
description: Honest evaluation of Agence against the multi-agent framework landscape as of March 2026. Agence has genuine architectural leads but is 12-18 months behind in execution maturity.
root_cause: No formal competitive analysis existed. Design decisions were made without reference to what others have shipped.
solution: Revisit this analysis each quarter. Prioritise the gaps that matter most: test suite, container isolation (v0.4), public documentation.

agence_unique_strengths: Git as the only coordination layer — no message queue, broker, or database. Unique in the space.
CODEX governance — hard constraints (LAWS) agents cannot override. No competitor has constitutional governance.
Blast-radius-aware model routing — cheapest capable model by task danger level. Novel cost/risk discipline.
Shell-first portability — bash + jq only. AutoGen/CrewAI require Python venv just to start.
Command prefix taxonomy (^ ~ ! + /) — typed instruction protocol between human and agents. Original design.
Structural security post-incident — guardrails designed at OS boundary, not advisory bash functions. Most frameworks don't consider this.
agence_current_gaps: Maturity: pre-alpha, single developer, recovered from AI incident that wiped v0.3
No GUI: AutoGen Studio, Cursor, Copilot all have UI. Agence is terminal-only.
No test suite: ShellSpec all pending. Most dangerous gap.
No Python/TS SDK: hard to integrate with the Python-heavy ML/LLM ecosystem.
Documentation is internal: no public onboarding story.
Container isolation planned (v0.4) but not built: security still advisory in practice until then.
competitive_summary.closest_competitor: OpenHands (formerly OpenDevin) — closest to Agence v0.4 vision with Docker sandbox per agent

name_clarifications.openclaw: unknown — possibly Cline (open-source Claude Dev, already in Agence router) or a typo
name_clarifications.openfang: likely OpenHands (formerly OpenDevin) — autonomous software dev agent, Python, sandboxed Docker execution, closest competitor to Agence v0.4

other_competitors_to_watch.name: Claude Code
other_competitors_to_watch.notes: Terminal-based autonomous agent, being integrated as !claude

other_competitors_to_watch.name: Goose
other_competitors_to_watch.notes: Open-source developer agent, terminal-first like Agence

other_competitors_to_watch.name: Plandex
other_competitors_to_watch.notes: AI coding engine for long-horizon tasks, terminal-based

verdict: Agence is a research-grade architecture project with genuinely novel ideas. The git-native coordination, CODEX governance, and blast_radius routing are intellectual leads on anything in the space. But it is 12-18 months behind in execution maturity relative to AutoGen/CrewAI. The v0.4 container-per-agent architecture — if built cleanly — would be meaningfully more secure than anything currently available in open source. That is the moment Agence becomes a credible alternative rather than an interesting prototype.
